metadata.name: Alpine Ski Slopes
metadata.description: 60 questions about the ski slopes of Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley (now Palisades Tahoe) — covering geography, history, culture, art, science, and racing.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: In which US state are both Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley ski resorts located?
questions.tidbits: The Alps span eight countries: France, Switzerland, Italy, Monaco, Liechtenstein, Austria, Germany, and Slovenia.

questions.questionText: What large alpine lake lies just a few miles east of Palisades Tahoe?
questions.tidbits: Chamonix hosted the first Winter Olympics in 1924, making it a historic landmark in alpine skiing history.

questions.questionText: What mountain range contains both Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley?
questions.tidbits: Verbier, Switzerland sits at over 1,500 meters elevation, giving it reliable snowfall from November through April.

questions.questionText: What small town on the north shore of Lake Tahoe sits just a few miles from Palisades Tahoe?
questions.tidbits: Val Thorens in France is the highest ski resort in the Alps at 2,300 meters, with skiing possible into May.

questions.questionText: What railroad town roughly 12 miles northwest of Lake Tahoe serves as a gateway city to Palisades Tahoe?
questions.tidbits: Innsbruck, Austria has hosted the Winter Olympics twice — in 1964 and 1976.

questions.questionText: The valley in which Squaw Valley sits is drained by which creek that flows into the Truckee River?
Approximately how many acres of skiable terrain does Palisades Tahoe (both villages combined) offer?
What is the base elevation in feet at the Alpine Meadows village?
At roughly what elevation in feet does the summit of KT-22 chair at Palisades Tahoe sit?
Which Nevada county border is Lake Tahoe's eastern shoreline closest to, making part of the lake lie in Nevada?
Which 1993 snowboard comedy film features the character Charles 'Chunk' Godfrey and was largely set at a ski resort?
questions.tidbits: The FIS Alpine Ski World Cup circuit was established in 1966 and features events across Europe, North America, and occasionally Asia.

questions.questionText: What iconic 1998 snowboard trick competition at the Winter X Games made halfpipe a mainstream spectator sport?
questions.tidbits: Lindsey Vonn holds the record for the most World Cup wins by a female alpine skier with 82 victories.

questions.questionText: Which legendary American skier nicknamed 'The Herminator' dominated World Cup racing in the late 1990s?
questions.tidbits: Mikaela Shiffrin surpassed Ingemar Stenmark's all-time World Cup win record in 2023, earning over 90 victories.

questions.questionText: Which American snowboarder won three Olympic halfpipe gold medals and trained extensively at Lake Tahoe resorts?
questions.tidbits: The Hahnenkamm downhill race in Kitzbühel, Austria is considered the most challenging race on the men's circuit.

questions.questionText: What 1969 ski film starring Robert Redford was filmed partly in the Sierra Nevada and helped romanticize downhill racing?
questions.tidbits: Giant slalom gates are wider than slalom gates — typically 4–8 meters wide versus 0.75 meters for slalom.

questions.questionText: Which Tahoe-area skier won back-to-back Olympic downhill golds in 2010 and 2014?
The 1960 Winter Olympics opening ceremony at Squaw Valley was narrated by which famous Disney figure?
Which ski film production company, known for films like 'Superheroes of Stoke,' frequently featured Palisades Tahoe riders in the 2010s?
Squaw Valley's 1960 Olympics were the first Winter Games to be broadcast live on television in the US — on which network?
Which Squaw Valley native and freestyle skier won the first-ever Olympic moguls gold medal in 1992 for the US?
In what year did Squaw Valley host the Winter Olympic Games? A) 1952, B) 1956, C) 1960, D) 1964
questions.tidbits: Skiing as a sport dates back thousands of years; ancient skis have been found in Norway and Russia dated to around 6000 BCE.

questions.questionText: What is the new combined name of Squaw Valley and Alpine Meadows since their 2021 rebranding?
questions.tidbits: The modern ski binding that releases during a fall was developed in the 1950s, dramatically reducing leg injuries.

questions.questionText: In what decade was Alpine Meadows ski resort originally founded?
questions.tidbits: Ski boots evolved from leather lace-up boots to the rigid plastic shell design in the 1960s, improving control significantly.

questions.questionText: In what year did Alpine Meadows open for its first ski season? A) 1958, B) 1961, C) 1965, D) 1968
questions.tidbits: Snowplowing (the "pizza" shape for beginners) was the dominant braking technique taught before parallel skiing became standard.

questions.questionText: What devastating natural disaster struck Alpine Meadows on March 31, 1982, killing seven people?
questions.tidbits: Moguls form naturally when many skiers make turns in the same spots, pushing snow into mounds over time.

questions.questionText: Which developer and entrepreneur originally built and opened Squaw Valley ski resort in 1949?
What company owns and operates Palisades Tahoe today, having acquired Squaw Valley Alpine Meadows in 2021?
The 1960 Squaw Valley Olympics were awarded to the resort over which other favored candidate city?
Who was the ski patrol dog whose handler, Frank Yeatman, survived the 1982 Alpine Meadows avalanche — the dog that helped locate a survivor named Anna Conrad?
In what year did KSL Capital Partners acquire Squaw Valley Ski Holdings, preceding the eventual Alterra merger? A) 2014, B) 2015, C) 2016, D) 2017
What iconic style of illustrated resort map, showing a bird's-eye perspective of the mountain with labeled runs, is called a what?
questions.tidbits: The iconic bright colors of ski gear emerged in the 1980s when synthetic materials replaced wool and made bold dyeing easy.

questions.questionText: What color symbol is used on North American ski trail signs to mark an intermediate run?
questions.tidbits: Après-ski culture — socializing after skiing — is especially prominent in Austrian and Swiss resorts like St. Anton.

questions.questionText: What shape and color symbol marks the easiest beginner runs on a North American ski trail map?
questions.tidbits: The ski lift was invented in Sun Valley, Idaho in 1936, inspired by banana-loading conveyors on cargo ships.

questions.questionText: Which legendary ski poster artist, known for his vibrant Art Deco mountain prints, created iconic imagery for resorts like Sun Valley in the mid-20th century?
questions.tidbits: Snowcats (tracked vehicles used for grooming) can cost over $500,000 each and operate through the night to prepare slopes.

questions.questionText: The official Palisades Tahoe trail map uses an oblique aerial illustration style. What term describes this artistic bird's-eye mountain perspective technique?
questions.tidbits: Modern ski helmets meet EN 1077 or ASTM F2040 safety standards and are designed to absorb impact at speeds up to 25 mph.

questions.questionText: What double black diamond symbol shape is used on North American ski maps to mark the most challenging expert-only terrain?
Palisades Tahoe's logo features a stylized mountain peak. What two colors dominate the resort's current branding?
Which Swiss graphic designer is widely credited with defining the clean, modernist aesthetic of mid-century European ski resort posters?
The famous illustrated trail maps of many Western ski resorts were produced for decades by which artist collective based in Colorado?
What photographic technique — using a long focal length lens to compress the apparent distance between ski runs — is commonly used in resort promotional photography?
What type of mechanical lift carries skiers up a mountain while they sit in chairs hanging from a moving cable?
questions.tidbits: At high speeds, alpine skiers generate G-forces in turns similar to those experienced by fighter jet pilots — up to 3G.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the crystalline solid water structure that forms naturally as snowflakes?
questions.tidbits: Piste grooming machines mix compressed snow with water to create a firm, consistent surface for racing.

questions.questionText: What is the term for artificially produced snow blown onto ski slopes using machines?
questions.tidbits: Modern carbon-fiber ski poles are engineered to flex slightly on impact, reducing wrist strain during high-speed turns.

questions.questionText: What explosive device is commonly used by ski patrol to trigger controlled avalanche releases before the slopes open?
questions.tidbits: Altitude sickness can affect skiers above 2,500 meters — symptoms include headache, nausea, and fatigue.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the system installed on ski boots and bindings that releases the boot during a fall to prevent leg fractures?
questions.tidbits: UV radiation increases about 10–12% for every 1,000 meters of elevation, making sunscreen essential on ski slopes.

questions.questionText: What type of high-speed enclosed gondola system connects the two Palisades Tahoe base areas — replacing the need to drive between resorts?
Snowmaking guns mix compressed air with water to produce snow. What minimum temperature in Fahrenheit is typically required for snowmaking to work?
What is the term for the specific type of persistent weak layer in a snowpack — a faceted crystal layer — that creates high avalanche danger days after a storm?
Palisades Tahoe uses a remote avalanche control system called Gazex. What propellant gas mixture is detonated in Gazex exploders to trigger avalanche releases?
What is the scientific term for the density measurement of snow — grams per cubic centimeter — that ski patrol uses to assess avalanche potential?
What is the famous expert chairlift and run at Palisades Tahoe known for steep, ungroomed terrain — abbreviated KT?
questions.tidbits: The downhill event is the fastest alpine skiing discipline, with top racers exceeding 150 km/h (93 mph).

questions.questionText: What symbol on a trail sign indicates an expert-only, most difficult ski run?
questions.tidbits: Slalom courses feature 55–75 gates for men and 40–60 gates for women, requiring rapid short-radius turns.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the international ski racing organization that sanctions World Cup events?
questions.tidbits: The combined event in alpine skiing merges a downhill run with one or two slalom runs, rewarding all-around ability.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the very steep, narrow expert chute at Alpine Meadows known for its intimidating pitch and tight walls?
questions.tidbits: A ski race course inspection (called "inspection") allows athletes to side-slip down the course slowly to memorize the line.

questions.questionText: KT-22 at Palisades Tahoe is named for how many kick turns a pioneer skier needed to descend the face. How many kick turns gave the run its name?
questions.tidbits: Ski racing suits are aerodynamically tested in wind tunnels and can shave tenths of a second off race times.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the terrain park at Palisades Tahoe's main mountain that features halfpipes, rails, and jump lines for freestyle skiers and snowboarders?
Alpine Meadows was home to a prominent women's World Cup downhill race in the 1970s and 80s. What is the name of the main race venue run?
Which American ski racer, a Lake Tahoe local, won a World Cup downhill on the KT-22 course in 1969 — one of the first American men to win a downhill?
What is the vertical drop in feet of the main Palisades (former Squaw Valley) mountain from summit to base? A) 2,200 feet, B) 2,500 feet, C) 2,850 feet, D) 3,300 feet
In competitive ski racing, what is the name of the discipline combining a downhill and giant slalom section into a single run, which was debuted at the 1960 Squaw Valley Olympics?
